selected_pmids.reason: Directly addresses dendritic spine morphogenesis and synaptic plasticity in hippocampal CA1 pyramidal neurons, linking spine changes to memory formation.
Investigates the role of autophagy in regulating dendritic spine volume, density, and morphology specifically in proximal and distal dendrites of CA1 pyramidal neurons.
Shows how Tweety-homolog 1 protein influences dendritic spine morphology and density in CA1 and CA3 pyramidal neurons, implying a role in structural neuronal plasticity.
Demonstrates preservation of dendritic spine density in CA1 pyramidal neurons under pathological conditions, highlighting molecular mechanisms relevant to spine maintenance.
Examines dendritic morphology changes in CA1 pyramidal neurons after brain ischemia and the beneficial effects of treatment, relevant for spine plasticity in CA1 cells.
